Diagnose and audit the TOS ecosystem itself. Sweeps every skill (MAINTAINER.md, synced
references, evals, routing membership), shared engines (importable), and routing integrity.
Reads the audit trail (Quality Ledger, runtime traces, minority reports) to surface recurring
failure patterns. Produces human-readable repair plans the teacher/developer reviews and
approves — nothing high-stakes is auto-applied.

Trigger phrases: "is the ecosystem healthy", "why did this skill fail", "audit the skills",
"diagnose the environment", "what needs updating for the new skill", "repair plan",
"check drift", "run health check", "what's broken", "scan all skills", "impact analysis
for renaming a skill", "tos_check".

Governs the SYSTEM, not classroom artifacts. Engine: shared/health/health.py (offline,
stdlib); orchestrator: tools/tos_check.py (runs all diagnostic tools and writes
tos_report.json + tos_report.md).

Five capabilities:
1. Scan readiness — sweep every skill + shared engine + routing; emit readiness score,
   blocking issues, and release-gate recommendation
2. Diagnose — read ledger/ledger.json + traces; surface recurring problems in plain language
   (connector failures, minority reports, validation failures)
3. Impact analysis — when a skill is added/renamed, list every ecosystem file that must
   update (routing.json, ROUTING_MODEL.md, routing-map.md, STATE.md, METRICS.md,
   artifact-types.json)
4. Repair plan — ordered severity-tagged plan (mechanical vs. judgment); you edit or approve
5. Validate outputs — artifact JSON schema check + governance / no-fabrication / no-real-PII
   rule catalog before the artifact ships

Do NOT use skill-health to author lessons, assessments, or IEP documents (wrong skill).
Do NOT use it to score classroom artifacts (use quality-review). human_review_required: true
on all reports and repair plans.

action.description: Action to perform. summary: sweep all skills + engines + routing; return readiness score and blocking issues. Equivalent to shared/health/health.py --summary. impact_analysis: for a named skill being added or renamed, list every ecosystem file that must change. Equivalent to health.py --impact <skill_name>. diagnose: read the audit trail (ledger, traces) and surface recurring failure patterns in plain language. Produces a diagnosis document. validate_artifact: check a specific artifact against its schema + governance rules (calls tools/validate_outputs.py). Returns violations and corrective actions. full_tos_check: run tools/tos_check.py — all 10 diagnostic groups (drift, version, caps, render, feeds, cache, seeds, registry, sources, security) and write tos_report.json + tos_report.md.

skill_name.description: For impact_analysis: the skill being added, renamed, or removed. Examples: "lesson-planner", "atom-standards-match", "my-new-skill".

traces_dir.description: For diagnose: directory containing saved runtime execution traces. Defaults to runtime/traces if it exists.

artifact.description: For validate_artifact: the artifact JSON string to validate.

artifact_schema.description: For validate_artifact: schema to validate against.

tos_check_groups.description: For full_tos_check: specific check groups to run. If omitted, all 10 groups run.

tos_check_timeout.description: For full_tos_check: per-check timeout in seconds. Defaults to 30.

out_dir.description: For full_tos_check: directory to write tos_report.json and tos_report.md. If omitted, results are returned inline.

function.name: skill_repair
function.description:
Apply an approved skill-health repair plan with the smallest durable change. Separates
mechanical fixes (safe to automate) from judgment fixes (always human-only). Shows a
plain-language approval summary before applying anything. On approval, applies ONLY safe
mechanical fixes, then re-runs the drift guard (tools/sync_check.py).

Trigger phrases: "apply the repair plan", "fix the broken skill", "patch this skill",
"run the approved fixes", "apply mechanical fixes", "skill repair dry run", "what would
the repair change", "execute the approved patch".

Workflow (patch-principles.md):
1. Confirm basis — prefer approved plan from skill-health; if none, produce diagnosis + stop
2. Prepare — read full skill; identify only files that truly need changes; snapshot before edit
3. Patch minimally — mechanical (safe, automate) vs. judgment (human-only); fix root cause
4. Validate — re-run sync_check.py, re-read changed files; call out anything untestable
5. Summarize for approval — plain language: what changed, why, what stayed, any risk

Dry-run by default: shows what would change. --apply performs ONLY safe mechanical fixes.
Judgment items (architectural changes, logic rewrites, new skill design) always stay human.

Never presents a patch as complete if risky parts could not be validated. If the real problem
is broad architectural drift, says so and recommends a larger refactor — does not hide it.

Do NOT use skill-repair to diagnose issues (use skill-health first). Do NOT use it to
redesign a skill from scratch. Do NOT use it to make judgment calls about which approach
is architecturally correct — those belong with the human developer.
human_review_required: true on all output.

action.description: Action to perform. dry_run (default): show exactly what would change, which items are mechanical vs. judgment, and the risk assessment — but change nothing. apply: perform ONLY the safe mechanical fixes from the approved plan; judgment items stay as a TODO list for the human. Re-runs sync_check.py after. summarize_plan: parse a repair plan and produce a plain-language approval summary without running any fixes.

repair_plan.description: The repair plan to execute. Accepts either: (a) a JSON repair plan object produced by skill-health's diagnose action, or (b) a path to a saved repair plan file (e.g. "reports/repair-plan-2026-06-27.json"), or (c) a plain-text description of what needs to be fixed (skill-repair will parse it into mechanical vs. judgment items and stop for your confirmation before proceeding).

skill_name.description: The specific skill to repair (e.g. "lesson-planner", "atom-standards-match"). When provided alongside a repair plan, validates that the plan targets the correct skill before applying any changes.

confirmation_acknowledged.description: For apply action: must be true to proceed with changes. Acts as an explicit acknowledgment that the human has reviewed the dry_run output and approved it. apply will refuse to run without this set to true.

snapshot_before.description: If true, save a snapshot of all target files before applying any changes. Snapshots go to ledger/repair-snapshots/<timestamp>/. Defaults to true for apply action.

stop_on_first_failure.description: If true, halt the apply run if any mechanical fix fails validation after application. Defaults to true (safer; prevents cascading partial repairs).

function.name: special_education_support
function.description:
Generate IDEA-compliant IEP goal drafts, accommodation and modification plans, behavior
support plans, transition planning documents, and progress notes for K-12 special education
professionals. Every output cites the relevant legal basis (IDEA §300, ADA, Section 504).
All outputs are DRAFTS for qualified professionals to review, edit, and approve — never final
documents.

Trigger phrases: "write an IEP goal for", "draft accommodations for", "create a behavior
support plan", "help with transition planning", "write progress notes", "draft a modification
plan", "accommodation vs modification", "IDEA-compliant goal".

Integrates with atom-iep-goal (SMART goal drafting), atom-differentiate (modification
layer), and quality-review (governance gate). Routes complex eligibility questions to a
human — this skill never determines eligibility.

LEGAL BOUNDARIES (non-negotiable, always stated in output):
- Never determines eligibility for special education services
- Never overrides or supplements an existing IEP without the IEP team's approval
- All output is a DRAFT for the IEP team to review, modify, and approve per IDEA procedures
- Never includes real student names, SSN, or identifying PII — placeholders only
- Progress data fields require teacher-supplied actual data — never fabricated
- Output always includes: "DRAFT — requires IEP team review and approval before use"
- human_review_required: true on every output

Do NOT use this skill to determine eligibility, make placement decisions, or produce legally
binding documents. Do NOT use it to write general lesson accommodations (use lesson-planner
with differentiation_profiles instead for whole-class differentiation).

request_type.description: Type of special education document to draft. iep_goal generates one SMART annual goal (use multiple calls for multiple goals); accommodation_plan generates the full accommodations section; modification_plan generates curriculum modification recommendations.

grade.description: Student's current grade level. Examples: "3rd grade", "9th grade", "K".

disability_category.description: IDEA disability category (optional — do not guess if unknown). Examples: "Specific Learning Disability", "Autism Spectrum Disorder", "Other Health Impairment", "Emotional Behavioral Disorder", "Intellectual Disability", "Speech/Language Impairment". This informs goal writing; does not determine eligibility.

domain.description: Domain for the goal or plan. Required for iep_goal. For progress_note or present_level, describe the domain in present_level_of_performance.

subject.description: Academic subject if domain is academic/reading/math/writing. Examples: "ELA", "Math".

standards.description: Standards the goal should connect to. Goals should tie to grade-level or near-grade-level standards even when modified. Provide FL B.E.S.T. codes when known.

present_level_of_performance.description: Current performance data: what the student can do, at what level, with what accuracy. Use placeholders for names (e.g. "the student", "Student A"). Required for iep_goal and behavior_support_plan. Never fabricate — provide real teacher-observed data or placeholder markers like "[INSERT ASSESSMENT DATA]".

annual_goal_focus.description: For iep_goal: the specific skill or behavior the goal targets over the coming year. Examples: "decode multisyllabic words", "initiate peer interactions", "manage frustration using a coping strategy".

measurement_criteria.description: For iep_goal: desired accuracy/frequency criterion (e.g. "80% accuracy across 3 of 4 trials", "4 out of 5 consecutive opportunities"). If omitted, a standard criterion is drafted with a placeholder.

behavior_of_concern.description: For behavior_support_plan: observable description of the behavior (topography, frequency, intensity, duration). Use objective language; no labels or diagnoses.

function_of_behavior.description: For behavior_support_plan: hypothesized function from FBA. Use unknown_requires_fba if an FBA has not been completed.

age_at_transition.description: For transition_plan: student's current age. Florida requires transition planning to begin by age 14 (or earlier if the IEP team determines appropriate).

post_secondary_goals.description: For transition_plan: post-secondary vision across domains. Keys: education, employment, independent_living. Values: plain-language goal statements from the student/family.

legal_notice_acknowledged.description: Must be true to proceed. Confirms the requestor understands all output is a DRAFT requiring IEP team review and approval, and that this skill never determines eligibility.

function.name: standards_updater
function.description:
Crawl, detect, verify, and report on changes to Florida K-12 standards corpora: B.E.S.T.,
NGSSS, CTE frameworks, Florida Statutes Title XLVIII (ch.1000-1013), State Board rules
(FAC 6A), FAST/B.E.S.T./EOC assessment fact sheets, graduation requirements (s.1003.4282 F.S.),
and WIDA/ELD standards. Produces a currency brief: what changed, why it matters, and what
downstream TOS artifacts need updating.

Trigger phrases: "check if standards have changed", "crawl FLDOE for updates", "are the
standards current", "what changed in Florida standards", "update the standards corpus",
"currency brief", "crawl CPALMS", "check the source URLs", "standards refresh", "is my
pacing guide still current".

Two detection modes:
- document discovery: new/changed files found via crawl_seeds in sources.json
- content-change monitoring: sha256 of watch_pages (statute/rule/guidance pages)

Change intelligence (triage → verify → brief):
1. Discover broadly — secondary sources are discovery only, never confirmation
2. Verify on PRIMARY source (CPALMS, FLDOE, FL Legislature, FAC/flrules.org, WIDA)
3. Score confidence: high (primary states it directly) / medium (scope needs interpretation)
   / low (partial access / ambiguity)
4. Brief the impact across 8 dimensions: standards, courses/curriculum, pacing/guidance,
   instructional materials, assessment, graduation, EL, policy/compliance

Render prong chain fires on ANY obstacle or confidence < 0.95 (not only js_required):
http_static → local_render → offline_docintel → screenshot_ocr → cloud_render (opt-in).
Multiple prongs aggregate; discrepancies → minority report. Wayback Machine CDX API
confirms genuine change vs. transient fetch failure.

Ethics: one honest User-Agent; robots.txt respected; no CAPTCHA bypass; no UA rotation.
If a source is unreachable or robots-disallowed: report and stop for that source.

Do NOT use standards-updater to write lesson plans or assessments. Do NOT use it to
auto-apply standards changes — every confirmed change is advisory and human-reviewed.
human_review_required: true on all outputs.

action.description: Action to perform. check: offline check against existing snapshot (no network fetch). crawl: live crawl of all seeds in sources.json; detect new/changed files. download: download accepted updates to out/ directory. report: generate currency brief from last crawl results. update_hashes: save new content sha256 baselines for watch_pages. source_currency: run source_currency.py to check if watched source URLs have gone stale, moved, been superseded, or disappeared (separate from file discovery). render_check: check render prong availability (offline capability check).

state.description: State standards corpus to target. Currently only Florida is implemented. Defaults to florida.

report_path.description: For report or crawl: path to write the JSON currency brief. Defaults to report.json.

out_dir.description: For download: directory to save downloaded files before they are moved into resources/<state>/<category>/. Defaults to out/.

max_retries.description: Maximum retries per URL, honoring Retry-After / RateLimit-Reset headers. Defaults to 3.

saturation_n.description: Stop crawling when no new documents appear in N consecutive seed fetches. Defaults to 5.

checkpoint_path.description: Path to save/load checkpoint file for resumable crawls. Required for --resume.

resume.description: Resume from the checkpoint at checkpoint_path. Defaults to false.

max_wait.description: Maximum seconds to wait on a single rate-limit backoff. Defaults to 300 (5 min).

no_render_fallback.description: Disable the render prong chain. By default, any obstacle or confidence < 0.95 triggers the chain automatically. Use this only for quick offline checks.

render_enable_cloud.description: Enable the cloud render prong (firecrawl). Off by default — the offline prongs (local_render, offline_docintel, screenshot_ocr) are always preferred.

confidence_threshold.description: Minimum confidence score (0.0-1.0) before the render prong chain fires. Defaults to 0.95 per the TOS render policy.

function.name: teacher_core
function.description:
TOS routing hub. Receives any K-12 educator request and returns a structured routing decision:
which TOS skill to invoke next, what parameters to pass it, and the confidence in that routing
choice. Use teacher_core when routing is ambiguous (overlapping skills) or when you want the
routing rationale recorded. Returns a minority report when two or more skills are plausible
within 10 points of confidence.

Trigger phrases: any educator request that does not obviously map to one skill — "help me with
a lesson", "I need something for my IEP meeting", "make me a quiz", "can you draft a parent
note", "audit my skills", "what skill should I use for this?"

Do NOT use teacher_core to produce the actual artifact — it routes only. After receiving the
routing decision, call the target skill with the parameters teacher_core recommends.

Routes to: lesson-planner, assessment-designer, special-education-support, family-communication,
meeting-classifier, quality-review, curriculum-mapping, intervention-mtss, presentation-builder,
professional-learning, school-administration, document-intelligence, output-validator,
feed-curator, standards-updater, teacher-profile, skill-health, skill-repair, and all
atom-* sub-skills.

Output always includes human_review_required: true and a metadata block.

request.description: The teacher's raw request, question, or description of what they need. Provide as much context as possible — grade, subject, student context, urgency, artifact type desired.

grade.description: Grade level hint (e.g. "3rd grade", "9-12", "K-2"). Helps narrow routing when multiple skills are plausible.

subject.description: Subject area hint (e.g. "ELA", "Algebra 1", "Biology"). Optional — extracted from request if not provided.

artifact_type_hint.description: Optional hint about the desired output type (e.g. "lesson plan", "quiz", "IEP goal", "parent email"). teacher_core will confirm or correct this from request context.

teacher_context.description: Optional background: teacher role, school, district, current unit, known student needs. Improves routing precision. Never include real student names or PII — placeholders only.

force_minority_report.description: If true, always emit a minority report showing all plausible routes with confidence scores, even when one route is clearly dominant.

function.name: teacher_profile
function.description:
Initialize, manage, and query the TOS teacher operating context: role, grade band, subject,
school, district, student demographics, local constraints, collaboration network, and system
preferences (offline tier, retrieval mode, feed update mode). Teacher-stated facts outrank
crawled inference in all downstream routing and artifact generation.

Trigger phrases: "set up my TOS profile", "update my profile", "what's in my profile",
"change my school", "reset my preferences", "I moved to a new school", "set my grade level",
"update my subject", "configure TOS for my class", "show my profile", "what does TOS know
about me", "teacher profile setup", "preferences wizard".

The local profile is stored in shared/context/profiles/ (gitignored — never committed).
Teacher-stated facts carry provenance: teacher_stated / confidence: high and outrank
any crawled or inferred context via shared/context/sot_resolver.py.

All preferences are REVERSIBLE:
- --preferences --reset clears preferences without deleting identity/role
- school_change re-runs school/standards scope questions
- Individual fields updatable without re-running the full wizard

System preferences (L0 roadmap):
- offline_tier: cached_python (default) | local_semantic | local_llm
- retrieval_mode: stdlib_keyword (always available) | vector (opt-in, gated on consent)
- feed_update_mode: manual (default) | on_session_start | scheduled

PRIVACY:
- All profile data is stored LOCALLY and gitignored — never committed to the repo
- Never includes real student names, SSNs, or PII — placeholders only
- Staff directory integration requires STAFF_INGEST_AUTHORIZED=true environment variable

Do NOT use teacher-profile to generate lesson plans, assessments, or IEP documents (route
those to the appropriate skill). Do NOT use it to read or modify the standards corpus
(use standards-updater).

action.description: Action to perform. init: run the full setup wizard (guided questions for role, grade, subject, school, student context, collaboration network, preferences). show: display current profile in readable format. validate: check profile completeness and flag missing fields that affect routing. register: write the current profile to the context overlay so downstream skills inherit it automatically. preferences: view or modify L0 system preferences (offline_tier, retrieval_mode, feed_update_mode) without re-running the full wizard. reset: clear preferences (with reset_scope); identity/role preserved unless scope=full. school_change: re-run school, district, and standards-scope questions when the teacher moves to a new school. Other preferences preserved. update_field: update a single profile field without running the full wizard. export_context_fragment: return the profile as a context fragment string suitable for prepending to any skill's system prompt.

field_name.description: For update_field: the profile field to update. Examples: "grade_band", "subject", "school_name", "district", "student_demographics", "co_teacher_present", "offline_tier", "feed_update_mode".

field_value.description: For update_field: the new value for the field. For boolean fields use "true"/"false". For array fields use comma-separated values (e.g. "ELL,IEP,gifted").

reset_scope.description: For reset: what to clear. preferences_only resets offline_tier/retrieval_mode/ feed_update_mode. school_and_standards_only resets school/district/standards scope. full_profile clears everything and prompts re-init.

preference_updates.description: For preferences action: key-value pairs to set. Keys: offline_tier, retrieval_mode, feed_update_mode, local_model. Example: {"offline_tier":"local_semantic", "feed_update_mode":"on_session_start"}.

teacher_context.description: For init or update: teacher context object. All fields optional — wizard prompts for missing required fields. Never include real student names or PII.

role.description: Teaching role (e.g. 'general education teacher', 'special education teacher', 'instructional coach', 'department head', 'principal')

district.description: District name (e.g. 'Orange County Public Schools', 'Miami-Dade County')

student_demographics.description: Anonymized class profile (e.g. '25 students, 30% ELL, 20% with IEPs, 1 gifted pull-out')

co_teacher_present.description: Whether a co-teacher is in the classroom
